question: [File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction whose `signedTransactionBytes` field contains a deeply nested or recursively structured protobuf payload that causes `SignedTransaction.parseFrom()` to consume unbounded CPU/memory during deserialization, crashing the importer processing thread and halting ingestion on that node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where `signedTransactionBytes` is non-empty but contains a maximally large protobuf blob, causing `TransactionBody.parseFrom(signedTransaction.getBodyBytes())` to allocate a multi-gigabyte object on the heap, triggering an OutOfMemoryError that kills the processing node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: parseTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a valid outer `SignedTransaction` wrapper but a malformed inner `bodyBytes` field, causing `TransactionBody.parseFrom()` to throw `InvalidProtocolBufferException` which is re-thrown as `ProtobufException`, propagating uncaught through the record file reader and crashing the importer pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction whose `TransactionBody` contains a maximally large unknown field payload, causing `Hex.encodeHexString(body.toByteArray())` in the error log path to allocate a very large string, exhausting heap memory on the processing node and causing it to crash?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a `TransactionBody` with zero unknown fields and `DATA_NOT_SET` data case, causing `log.error()` to be called with `Hex.encodeHexString(body.toByteArray())` for every such transaction in a batch, flooding the logging subsystem and causing I/O saturation that degrades or crashes the processing node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: parseTransactionType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with multiple unknown fields in `TransactionBody` (not exactly one), causing `log.error()` to be invoked with the full hex-encoded body on every such transaction, and by submitting many such transactions in a record file, cause repeated large string allocations that exhaust JVM heap and crash the importer?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with a very long chain of sibling transactions all having `ContractCallResult` set (but none with `contractNum == HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM`), causing the `while (candidateRecord != null && candidateRecord != parent)` loop to iterate over thousands of entries per transaction, consuming excessive CPU and stalling the processing node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file where every transaction has a `ContractCallResult` with a `contractNum` that is never `HOOK_CONTRACT_NUM` (365), causing `parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()` to traverse the entire `previous` chain for each new transaction, resulting in O(n^2) processing complexity that stalls or crashes the importer node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with a maximally long `previous` chain where each `RecordItem` holds a large `ContractFunctionResult`, causing the `while` loop in `parsePossiblyHookContractRelatedParent()` to retain all prior `RecordItem` objects in memory simultaneously, exhausting heap and crashing the processing node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: nextHookContext()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a deeply nested parent-child transaction chain where each child's `hookExecutionQueue` is null, causing `nextHookContext()` to recursively call `parent.nextHookContext()` through hundreds of levels, triggering a `StackOverflowError` that crashes the processing thread?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: nextHookContext()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a CryptoTransfer transaction that triggers a deeply nested hook execution chain where `hookExecutionQueue` is null at each level, causing unbounded recursive calls to `parent.nextHookContext()` and a `StackOverflowError` that terminates the importer JVM process?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: addContractTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction that references an extremely large number of unique `EntityId` values, causing `getContractTransactions()` to grow the `contractTransactions` `HashMap` without bound, exhausting heap memory and crashing the processing node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: addContractTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a contract transaction where `contractTransactionPredicate` accepts all entity IDs and the transaction references thousands of unique contract IDs, causing repeated `computeIfAbsent` calls on the `contractTransactions` `HashMap` to allocate thousands of `ContractTransaction` objects per record item, exhausting heap across many such transactions?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: populateContractTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that populates `contractTransactions` with a very large number of entries, causing `populateContractTransactions()` to create a large `ArrayList` of all keys and then call `setContractIds(ids)` on every value, resulting in excessive memory allocation and GC pressure that degrades or crashes the processing node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: populateContractTransactions()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction that causes `populateContractTransactions()` to set the same mutable `ids` `ArrayList` reference on all `ContractTransaction` objects, and if any downstream code mutates this shared list, cause data corruption that leads to incorrect state being persisted and processing errors that crash subsequent nodes?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: doAddEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that causes `doAddEntityId()` to be called with a very large number of unique `EntityId` values, growing the `entityTransactions` `HashMap` without bound and exhausting heap memory on the processing node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: doAddEntityId()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction where the shared `entityTransactionBuilder` is lazily initialized once and then reused across all `computeIfAbsent` calls, causing a race condition if `doAddEntityId()` is called concurrently from multiple threads, resulting in corrupted `EntityTransaction` objects and processing failures that crash the node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAndIncrementLogIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction that generates an extremely large number of log events, causing `getAndIncrementLogIndex()` to increment the `AtomicInteger` past `Integer.MAX_VALUE`, wrapping to negative values and causing downstream log index validation failures that crash the processing pipeline?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getAndIncrementLogIndex()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a contract transaction with a maximally large number of EVM log events, causing `getAndIncrementLogIndex()` to be called thousands of times per `RecordItem`, and by submitting many such transactions in a single record file, cause excessive `AtomicInteger` contention and CPU spin that degrades the processing node?

[File: 'File Name: common/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/common/domain/transaction/RecordItem.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: parseParent()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a record file with a batch transaction where `parentTimestamp.equals(previous.parent.transactionRecord.getParentConsensusTimestamp())` is true but `previous.parent.parent` is null, causing `parseParent()` to return null and silently break the parent-child invariant, leading to incorrect state processing that corrupts the mirror node database?
